 {"id":"bd-1q0e","title":"WS4.3: Add comprehensive tests for multi-command classification","description":"Comprehensive test suite for multi-command classification (WS4):

1. SPLIT_COMMANDS UNIT TESTS:

   Basic splitting:
   - "cargo build" → ["cargo build"]
   - "cargo fmt && cargo build" → ["cargo fmt", "cargo build"]
   - "cd /proj && cmake .. && make" → ["cd /proj", "cmake ..", "make"]
   - "make || echo fail" → ["make", "echo fail"]
   - "cd /tmp && make -j4 || echo fail" → ["cd /tmp", "make -j4", "echo fail"]
   - "a ; b ; c" → ["a", "b", "c"]
   - " && cargo build" → ["", "cargo build"] or ["cargo build"] (decide behavior)
   - "cargo build --release" → ["cargo build --release"]

2. QUOTING EDGE CASES:

   Single quotes:
   - "echo '&&' && cargo build" → ["echo '&&'", "cargo build"]
   - "echo 'hello;world' && make" → ["echo 'hello;world'", "make"]

   Double quotes:
   - "echo \"&&\" && cargo build" → ["echo \"&&\"", "cargo build"]
   - "echo \"it's && done\" && make" → ["echo \"it's && done\"", "make"]

   Backticks (legacy command substitution):
   - "echo \`date\` && cargo build" → ["echo \`date\`", "cargo build"]
   - Document if backtick-nested && is handled or not

   Escaped quotes:
   - "echo \\\"hello && cargo build" → behavior depends on shell, document choice

   ANSI-C quoting ($'...'):
   - "$'hello\\nworld' && cargo build" — document if supported

3. CLASSIFICATION INTEGRATION TESTS:

   Should classify as COMPILATION:
   - "cargo fmt && cargo build" → is_compilation: true
   - "cd /project && make -j8" → is_compilation: true
   - "export RUSTFLAGS='-C opt-level=3' && cargo build --release" → is_compilation: true
   - "mkdir -p build && cmake -B build && cmake --build build" → is_compilation: true
   - "echo 'Starting...' && cargo test" → is_compilation: true (second segment)

   Should classify as NON-COMPILATION:
   - "echo hello && echo world" → is_compilation: false
   - "ls -la && cat file.txt" → is_compilation: false
   - "git status && git log" → is_compilation: false

4. FAIL-OPEN EDGE CASES:

   Malformed input (should NOT panic, return usable result):
   - Unclosed single quote: "echo 'hello && cargo build"
   - Unclosed double quote: "echo \"hello && cargo build"
   - Unclosed backtick: "echo \`date && cargo build"
   - Empty string: ""
   - Only whitespace: "   "
   - Very long input (10KB+): verify no timeout/panic
   - Input with null bytes: "cargo\x00build" (if applicable)
   - Unicode: "echo '你好' && cargo build"

5. REGRESSION TESTS:
   - All existing classification tests must still pass
   - Single-command classification behavior unchanged
   - Performance: classify_command("cargo fmt && cargo build") completes in <5ms

6. PIPE PRESERVATION:
   - "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" → single segment (pipe is NOT a command separator)
   - "make 2>&1 | grep error && echo done" → ["make 2>&1 | grep error", "echo done"]

Test location: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (#[cfg(test)] module)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:09.996516181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:27:07.454725355Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:27:07.454648613Z","close_reason":"Added 17 comprehensive tests for multi-command classification: split edge cases (3-segment chain, single with flags, nested quotes, pipe+and combo, leading operator), classification integration (6 compilation cases including cargo fmt+build, cd+make, export+cargo, cmake chain, echo+test, semicolon chain; 3 non-compilation cases), performance test (100 iterations <5ms each), and pipe preservation tests. Total test count now 1188, all passing, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-16t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-1qt9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-9ka6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q3p","title":"Idea: Soft-fail artifact retrieval with actionable diff","description":"## Background
Artifact retrieval can fail even if remote build succeeded (rsync issues, permissions). Hard failure can confuse users if build output already streamed. A softer error with actionable detail improves UX.

## Goals
- Detect artifact retrieval failures and present clear remediation.
- Keep fail-open semantics for subsequent commands.

## Design / Approach
- On artifact retrieval failure, log which patterns were attempted and rsync stderr snippet.
- Return a warning and allow continuation (no denial change for already executed commands).
- Provide `rch diagnose` suggestion to re-run with verbose or fetch artifacts manually.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Enhance error classification for retrieve_artifacts.
- Add warning display + JSON fields for automation.
- Update docs/troubleshooting.

## Tests
- Unit: error classification includes attempted patterns.
- Integration: mock retrieval failure yields warning (not panic).
- E2E: warning displayed in verbose mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Retrieval failure is reported clearly without crashing.
- User sees actionable next steps.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Remote success but artifact retrieval fails -> warn but keep deny.
- Include attempted patterns + rsync stderr snippet.
- Provide actionable follow-up steps in message.

## E2E Outline
- Mock retrieval failure -> warning emitted with patterns.
- Exit code unchanged from remote success path.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: artifact retrieval failure classification.
- rch/src/hook.rs: warning path does not alter deny/allow semantics.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh: mock retrieval failure -> warning + actionable info.
","notes":"## Implementation Complete (EmeraldMoose)

### Changes Made:
1. **Added ArtifactRetrievalWarning struct** (rch/src/error.rs)
   - Captures attempted patterns, rsync stderr snippet, exit code
   - Includes actionable suggestions
   - Provides format_warning() for human-readable output
   - Provides to_json() for machine-readable output
   - Implements Display trait

2. **Updated artifact retrieval error handling** (rch/src/hook.rs)
   - Extracts rsync exit code from error message
   - Creates structured ArtifactRetrievalWarning on failure
   - Shows detailed warning in verbose/interactive mode
   - Outputs JSON in machine mode for automation
   - Maintains soft-fail semantics (compilation still succeeds)

3. **Added 6 unit tests** for ArtifactRetrievalWarning:
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_basic
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_format
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_json
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_truncates_stderr
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_with_custom_suggestions
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_display_trait

### Remaining work:
- E2E test script (scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh) - optional
- Update docs/troubleshooting - optional

### Verification:
- cargo check passes
- All 6 new tests pass
- 1099 total tests in rch package pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:55:32.592525740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:12:05.635332867Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:12:05.635214798Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1qfm","title":"Add code coverage reporting (cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov)","description":"**Add code coverage reporting with cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov**

## Scope
Integrate code coverage tooling into the CI pipeline:
- Choose between cargo-tarpaulin (easier) or llvm-cov (more accurate)
- Generate coverage reports in multiple formats (HTML, LCOV, JSON)
- Upload to coverage service (Codecov or Coveralls)

## Requirements
- Coverage for all crates (rch, rchd, rch-common, rch-wkr, rch-telemetry)
- Exclude test code from coverage metrics
- Generate per-file and per-function coverage
- Integration with GitHub Actions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coverage reports generated on every CI run
- [ ] HTML report viewable as artifact
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Per-crate coverage breakdown
- [ ] Historical coverage tracking","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteDune","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:03:31.044005621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:10.481769932Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:10.481689001Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria verified: CI generates LCOV/HTML reports, Codecov badge in README, per-crate summary via coverage-summary.md, test code excluded via .cargo/config.toml","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qfm","depends_on_id":"bd-2mc4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1qhj","title":"Add UX regression suite for human-facing reliability diagnostics","description":"Add a dedicated UX-quality regression suite for human-facing reliability outputs (status/doctor/failure/remediation narratives). Ensure operators and users consistently get clear, safe, and actionable guidance even as underlying reliability logic evolves.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Golden snapshot tests cover human-readable outputs for healthy, degraded, quarantined, fail-open, compatibility-mismatch, and rollback/canary-gate scenarios.
- Tests assert each non-healthy output contains: clear diagnosis summary, reason code(s), concrete remediation command(s), and explicit risk/safety note when destructive actions are involved.
- Output quality checks enforce deterministic ordering and wording constraints that avoid ambiguous or contradictory operator instructions.
- Redaction checks verify human-facing outputs never leak secrets/PII from logs/artifacts while still preserving troubleshooting utility.
- End-to-end scripts validate status+doctor UX across representative mixed-failure workflows with detailed structured phase logs and attached rendered output artifacts.
- CI/nightly include UX-regression tier and fail on unreviewed narrative drift.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:50.590771061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:52.509262602Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","observability","quality","rch-rel-2026q1","testing","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.709632957Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.304904309Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.505552174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:01.908121239Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.105694778Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:52.305725898Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.913475714Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:52.096624595Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"User-outcome rationale: machine-schema tests alone do not protect operator comprehension. This bead makes human remediation quality a release-gated reliability contract with deterministic tests and detailed logging artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:03Z"},{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: UX regression scenarios now explicitly consume the shared E2E harness (bd-vvmd.7.7) and integrated reliability script suite (bd-vvmd.7.2) so snapshot assertions reflect real operational workflows, not isolated mocks.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:52Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1qhj","title":"Add UX regression suite for human-facing reliability diagnostics","description":"Add a dedicated UX-quality regression suite for human-facing reliability outputs (status/doctor/failure/remediation narratives). Ensure operators and users consistently get clear, safe, and actionable guidance even as underlying reliability logic evolves.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Golden snapshot tests cover human-readable outputs for healthy, degraded, quarantined, fail-open, compatibility-mismatch, and rollback/canary-gate scenarios.
- Tests assert each non-healthy output contains: clear diagnosis summary, reason code(s), concrete remediation command(s), and explicit risk/safety note when destructive actions are involved.
- Output quality checks enforce deterministic ordering and wording constraints that avoid ambiguous or contradictory operator instructions.
- Redaction checks verify human-facing outputs never leak secrets/PII from logs/artifacts while still preserving troubleshooting utility.
- End-to-end scripts validate status+doctor UX across representative mixed-failure workflows with detailed structured phase logs and attached rendered output artifacts.
- UX regressions include parity assertions that human remediation narratives remain consistent across workers and local-vs-remote execution outcomes for equivalent scenarios.
- CI/nightly include UX-regression tier and fail on unreviewed narrative drift.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:50.590771061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:13:40.785510648Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","observability","quality","rch-rel-2026q1","testing","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.709632957Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.304904309Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.505552174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:01.908121239Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.105694778Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:13:20.831513424Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:13:23.509563789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:52.305725898Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:02.913475714Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:52.096624595Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"User-outcome rationale: machine-schema tests alone do not protect operator comprehension. This bead makes human remediation quality a release-gated reliability contract with deterministic tests and detailed logging artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:03Z"},{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: UX regression scenarios now explicitly consume the shared E2E harness (bd-vvmd.7.7) and integrated reliability script suite (bd-vvmd.7.2) so snapshot assertions reflect real operational workflows, not isolated mocks.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:52Z"},{"id":200,"issue_id":"bd-1qhj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightening: UX quality now explicitly requires cross-worker and local-vs-remote parity suites (bd-vvmd.7.10, bd-vvmd.7.11). This prevents contradictory human remediation guidance when infrastructure parity drifts while machine-level checks still pass.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:13:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qt9","title":"WS4.2: Integrate sub-command classification into classify_command()","description":"Modify the classification flow to handle multi-command strings:

Option A (recommended): Modify classify_command() at line 243:
  1. Call split_shell_commands(cmd)
  2. If only 1 segment → proceed as before (no behavior change)
  3. If multiple segments → classify each through the full pipeline
  4. Return the "most significant" result:
     - If ANY segment is compilation → return compilation (with highest confidence)
     - If ALL segments are non-compilation → return non-compilation

Option B: Modify check_structure() to skip &&/||/; rejection and instead return a "multi-command" indicator that the caller handles.

Option A is cleaner because it keeps the split logic at the top level.

IMPORTANT: The original command string is passed to the worker unchanged. We only split for classification purposes. The worker executes the full "cargo fmt && cargo build" as-is.

RECURSIVE CLASSIFICATION: When classifying sub-commands, use the same classify_command() function. This means sub-commands go through all 5 tiers. Guard against infinite recursion by passing a depth parameter or a flag that prevents re-splitting.

File: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (classify_command at line 243, check_structure at line 515)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:08.232803684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:08:14.733082845Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:08:14.733013386Z","close_reason":"Integrated multi-command splitting into classify_command(). Added classify_command_inner with depth guard (MAX_CLASSIFY_DEPTH=1) to prevent infinite recursion. Multi-command strings are split via split_shell_commands, each sub-command classified through full 5-tier pipeline, most significant compilation result returned. Updated 8 tests across patterns.rs, patterns_security_test.rs, proptest_tests.rs, e2e_hook.rs to reflect new behavior. All tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qt9","depends_on_id":"bd-2myq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qt9","depends_on_id":"bd-9ka6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1r8b","title":"Write unit tests for OutputContext detection","description":"# Write unit tests for OutputContext detection

## Task Description

Create comprehensive unit tests for the OutputContext detection system. This is CRITICAL because incorrect detection breaks agent compatibility.

## Background

OutputContext detection must correctly identify:
1. Hook mode (agents calling rch as subprocess)
2. Interactive mode (human at terminal)
3. Machine mode (--json flag)
4. Plain mode (piped output, NO_COLOR)
5. Colored mode (FORCE_COLOR without TTY)

Getting this wrong has severe consequences:
- False positive hook detection: No output for humans
- False negative hook detection: BREAKS AGENT JSON PROTOCOL

## Test Implementation

```rust
// rch-common/src/ui/context_tests.rs (or inline in context.rs)

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use std::env;

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE TESTS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    #[test]
    fn test_no_color_disables_colors() {
        // NO_COLOR standard: https://no-color.org/
        env::set_var("NO_COLOR", "1");

        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert!(!ctx.supports_color(), "NO_COLOR should disable colors");
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Plain);

        env::remove_var("NO_COLOR");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_no_color_empty_string() {
        // NO_COLOR with empty string should still disable
        env::set_var("NO_COLOR", "");

        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        // Per no-color.org spec, presence of var (even empty) disables color
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Plain);

        env::remove_var("NO_COLOR");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_force_color_levels() {
        // FORCE_COLOR can be 0, 1, 2, or 3
        env::set_var("FORCE_COLOR", "0");
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        // 0 means no color
        assert!(!ctx.supports_color() || ctx == OutputContext::Plain);
        env::remove_var("FORCE_COLOR");

        env::set_var("FORCE_COLOR", "1");
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert!(ctx.supports_color() || ctx == OutputContext::Interactive);
        env::remove_var("FORCE_COLOR");

        env::set_var("FORCE_COLOR", "3");
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert!(ctx.supports_color() || ctx == OutputContext::Interactive);
        env::remove_var("FORCE_COLOR");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_rch_json_env() {
        env::set_var("RCH_JSON", "1");

        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Machine);
        assert!(ctx.is_machine());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_rich());

        env::remove_var("RCH_JSON");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_rch_hook_mode_env() {
        env::set_var("RCH_HOOK_MODE", "1");

        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Hook);
        assert!(ctx.is_machine());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_rich());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_color());

        env::remove_var("RCH_HOOK_MODE");
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // CONTEXT PROPERTY TESTS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    #[test]
    fn test_hook_context_properties() {
        let ctx = OutputContext::Hook;

        assert!(ctx.is_machine());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_rich());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_color());
        assert!(!ctx.is_decorated());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_machine_context_properties() {
        let ctx = OutputContext::Machine;

        assert!(ctx.is_machine());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_rich());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_color());
        assert!(!ctx.is_decorated());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_interactive_context_properties() {
        let ctx = OutputContext::Interactive;

        assert!(!ctx.is_machine());
        assert!(ctx.supports_rich());
        assert!(ctx.supports_color());
        assert!(ctx.is_decorated());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_colored_context_properties() {
        let ctx = OutputContext::Colored;

        assert!(!ctx.is_machine());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_rich()); // Colors but no tables/panels
        assert!(ctx.supports_color());
        assert!(ctx.is_decorated());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_plain_context_properties() {
        let ctx = OutputContext::Plain;

        assert!(!ctx.is_machine());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_rich());
        assert!(!ctx.supports_color());
        assert!(!ctx.is_decorated());
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // UNICODE SUPPORT TESTS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    #[test]
    fn test_unicode_support_with_utf8_lang() {
        env::set_var("LANG", "en_US.UTF-8");
        // Note: This test depends on OutputContext being Interactive
        // In test environment, may not be true
        env::remove_var("LANG");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_unicode_support_with_dumb_term() {
        env::set_var("TERM", "dumb");

        let ctx = OutputContext::Interactive; // Force for test
        assert!(!ctx.supports_unicode());

        env::remove_var("TERM");
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // HOOK DETECTION TESTS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    #[test]
    fn test_is_known_subcommand() {
        assert!(OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("status"));
        assert!(OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("workers"));
        assert!(OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("daemon"));
        assert!(OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("config"));
        assert!(OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("hook"));
        assert!(OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("install"));
        assert!(OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("doctor"));

        // Not subcommands
        assert!(!OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("random"));
        assert!(!OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("--help"));
        assert!(!OutputContext::is_known_subcommand("-v"));
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // PRIORITY TESTS (order matters!)
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    #[test]
    fn test_rch_json_takes_priority_over_force_color() {
        env::set_var("RCH_JSON", "1");
        env::set_var("FORCE_COLOR", "3");

        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Machine, "RCH_JSON should win");

        env::remove_var("RCH_JSON");
        env::remove_var("FORCE_COLOR");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_hook_mode_takes_priority_over_force_color() {
        env::set_var("RCH_HOOK_MODE", "1");
        env::set_var("FORCE_COLOR", "3");

        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Hook, "Hook mode should win");

        env::remove_var("RCH_HOOK_MODE");
        env::remove_var("FORCE_COLOR");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_no_color_takes_priority_over_force_color() {
        env::set_var("NO_COLOR", "1");
        env::set_var("FORCE_COLOR", "3");

        // When both set, NO_COLOR wins per convention
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Plain, "NO_COLOR should win");

        env::remove_var("NO_COLOR");
        env::remove_var("FORCE_COLOR");
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // EDGE CASE TESTS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    #[test]
    fn test_empty_env_vars_handled() {
        // Should not panic with empty values
        env::set_var("FORCE_COLOR", "");
        let _ = OutputContext::detect();
        env::remove_var("FORCE_COLOR");

        env::set_var("TERM", "");
        let _ = OutputContext::detect();
        env::remove_var("TERM");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_invalid_force_color_values() {
        // Non-numeric FORCE_COLOR should be treated as truthy
        env::set_var("FORCE_COLOR", "yes");
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        // Should still enable colors
        assert!(ctx.supports_color() || ctx != OutputContext::Plain);
        env::remove_var("FORCE_COLOR");
    }
}
```

## Test Coverage Matrix

| Scenario | Expected Context | Properties |
|----------|------------------|------------|
| NO_COLOR=1 | Plain | no color, no rich |
| FORCE_COLOR=1 + no TTY | Colored | color, no rich |
| RCH_JSON=1 | Machine | no output |
| RCH_HOOK_MODE=1 | Hook | no output |
| TTY on stderr | Interactive | color + rich |
| No TTY, no env vars | Plain | no color, no rich |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] All environment variable tests pass
2. [ ] Context property tests pass
3. [ ] Priority/precedence tests pass
4. [ ] Edge case tests pass
5. [ ] No false positives in hook detection
6. [ ] No panics with malformed env vars
7. [ ] cargo test passes

This is the anti-regression backstop for the reliability architecture.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:22Z"},{"id":154,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Suggested closure checklist: (1) path-dep remote success validated against multi-repo fixtures, (2) repo convergence policy+allowlist enforced, (3) disk-pressure and process-triage loops proven under fault injection, (4) latency/SLO budgets passing, (5) docs+operator commands complete, (6) feature flags and rollback drill validated.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:46Z"},{"id":160,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Gate expanded to require: repo-convergence deep tests (bd-vvmd.3.8), output contract tests (bd-vvmd.6.8), full requirement-to-test matrix (bd-vvmd.7.8), and long-duration soak evidence (bd-vvmd.7.9).","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:52Z"},{"id":167,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Gate tightened again after plan audit: must include secure convergence credentials (3.9), predictive disk headroom controls (4.7), heartbeat-based stuck detection precision (5.7), reliability doctor UX surface (6.9), and cross-worker parity validation (7.10).","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:41Z"},{"id":169,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Release-gate rationale: closure requires evidence from unit/integration/e2e/fault-injection/soak/parity, schema-contract checks, performance budget checks, rollout/rollback readiness, and operator documentation. This bead is intentionally dependency-heavy to prevent partial reliability rollouts.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:48:13Z"},{"id":172,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Gate tightening update: release now requires local-vs-remote parity evidence () and deterministic replay readiness () to ensure user-visible correctness and faster incident root-cause workflows.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:53:03Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Gate tightening update: release now requires local-vs-remote parity evidence from bd-vvmd.7.11 and deterministic replay readiness from bd-vvmd.7.12 to ensure user-visible correctness and faster incident root-cause workflows.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:53:08Z"},{"id":178,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Release gate intent tightened: closure now requires evidence that command interception safety (`bd-vvmd.2.9`) and diagnostics redaction/retention (`bd-vvmd.6.10`) are both validated, in addition to parity/replay/fault/soak/schema coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:17Z"},{"id":182,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Release gate expanded: bd-vvmd.6.11 is mandatory evidence to prevent shipping helper-integration contract drift regressions.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:51Z"},{"id":189,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Release gate expansion: UX-regression bead bd-1qhj is mandatory evidence so human diagnostics/remediation guidance cannot regress silently.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:04Z"},{"id":197,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Release gate expansion: cancellation reliability bead bd-1yt6 is mandatory evidence (including unit/integration/e2e logs) before sign-off.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.7.6","title":"Add fault-injection E2E scenarios for partial failure and recovery","description":"Create deterministic fault-injection scenarios covering network interruptions, partial repo sync, timeout races, low-disk + stuck-process overlap, and daemon restarts during active builds. Validate graceful fail-open behavior, cleanup guarantees, and observability signal quality.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fault scenarios run on shared harness with reproducible seeds and stable log schema.
- Each injected fault has explicit expected decision/fallback assertions and remediation code checks.
- Cleanup invariants are verified post-scenario (worker slots, drift state, process debt, disk policy state).
- Output artifacts are retained for replay and postmortem analysis.
- Scenario set is integrated into CI/nightly reliability tiers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:25.654242643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:28.073835275Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fault-injection","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:30.644293543Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: reliability claims are only credible if fault-injection scenarios prove graceful recovery under compound failures.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","title":"Build reusable reliability E2E harness foundation","description":"Build a reusable reliability E2E harness that can orchestrate worker setup, repo fixture staging, induced fault hooks, and structured phase logging. This task establishes test infrastructure used by all scenario-specific E2E suites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic runner exists for setup/execute/verify/cleanup phases with stable JSON log schema.
- Harness supports worker lifecycle hooks (pre/post checks, remote command probes, cleanup verification).
- Harness supports injected failure hooks (network cut, sync timeout, partial update, daemon restart) behind explicit flags.
- Harness artifacts are persisted and indexed by scenario id for postmortem debugging.
- Existing scenario tasks (`bd-vvmd.2.8`, `bd-vvmd.3.6`, `bd-vvmd.4.6`, `bd-vvmd.5.6`) can consume harness primitives without bespoke scaffolding.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:32:11.226488884Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:36:29.093372917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","rch-rel-2026q1","scripts","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.326595217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:36:29.093247753Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":158,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This task resolves prior plan coupling where scenario beads depended directly on final script assembly. It introduces explicit harness infrastructure as the shared dependency, improving sequencing and reducing ambiguous ownership.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:48Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.7.8","title":"Create reliability requirement-to-test coverage matrix","description":"Produce a single authoritative reliability coverage matrix mapping each requirement/failure mode to concrete unit/integration/e2e tests, expected log assertions, and responsible bead. This prevents blind spots and makes release readiness auditable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Matrix covers path dependencies, repo convergence, storage pressure, process triage, fail-open semantics, and rollback/canary controls.
- Every matrix row references at least one executable test and one log/assertion artifact.
- Gaps are represented as explicit backlog items (no silent omissions).
- Matrix is CI-checked for staleness (fails when referenced tests are removed or renamed).
- Release gate (`bd-vvmd.7.5`) references matrix as mandatory closure It is the anti-blind-spot mechanism and directly strengthens release confidence.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:48Z"},{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage matrix requirement expanded: include explicit rows for command-classification safety (`bd-vvmd.2.9`), redaction/retention governance (`bd-vvmd.6.10`), local-vs-remote parity (`bd-vvmd.7.11`), and deterministic replay (`bd-vvmd.7.12`). Every row must map requirement -> tests -> logging assertions -> owning bead.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:17Z"},{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Matrix scope expansion: add explicit requirement rows for cross-project helper contract compatibility (bd-vvmd.6.11), including version-matrix checks, structured mismatch diffs, and fallback-behavior assertions.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:05:10Z"},{"id":190,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage matrix expansion: include explicit rows for human-facing diagnostic UX quality from bd-1qhj, including scenario snapshots, remediation-command presence, and reason-code clarity assertions.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:04Z"},{"id":198,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage matrix expansion: include dedicated cancellation rows for state-machine correctness, cleanup invariants, and mixed-failure e2e scenarios from bd-1yt6.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51Z"},{"id":201,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage-matrix scope tightened: added explicit dependencies on cross-worker parity (bd-vvmd.7.10), long-duration soak validation (bd-vvmd.7.9), and performance-budget evidence (bd-vvmd.6.6) so the matrix reflects complete executable evidence instead of partial requirement mapping.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:13:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","title":"Add long-duration concurrency soak E2E reliability suite","description":"Add long-duration, high-concurrency reliability soak tests that repeatedly run remote builds while injecting worker churn (restarts, temporary unreachability), disk-pressure transitions, and stale-process recovery events. Goal is to catch flapping behavior and cumulative state leaks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Soak suite runs for sustained duration with configurable concurrency and deterministic random seed.
- Assertions verify no unbounded leak in worker slots, stale process debt, or convergence drift counters.
- Fail-open fallback rate is measured and compared against budget thresholds.
- Structured logs include per-iteration decisions, fallback reasons, and worker health snapshots.
- CI/nightly integration publishes summary artifact with pass/fail thresholds and top regression deltas.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:32:43.083813828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:39:22.851895969Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","performance","rch-rel-2026q1","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:34.121974604Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:34.335754748Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:34.548615509Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:36:29.119112283Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Soak testing was added to surface cumulative-state and flapping issues that short E2E runs miss. This improves real-world user outcomes under sustained multi-agent load.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-w50b","title":"EPIC: TUI Visual Polish and Accessibility Improvements","description":"# TUI Visual Polish and Accessibility Improvements

## Overview
The RCH TUI dashboard (rch dashboard / rch tui) provides real-time monitoring of workers, builds, and system status. While functional, it has several visual polish and accessibility issues that prevent it from feeling premium/Stripe-level quality.

## Why This Matters
1. **Terminal Compatibility**: Using emoji instead of unicode breaks display in many terminals (PuTTY, older xterm, screen/tmux without utf8, Windows cmd)
2. **Accessibility**: Users with visual impairments or using screen readers need consistent, predictable iconography
3. **Discoverability**: Hidden features (like filter mode) reduce perceived quality and user efficiency
4. **Muscle Memory**: Missing vim keybindings frustrates power users who expect h/j/k/l navigation

## Key Files
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs - Widget rendering, icon definitions
- rch/src/tui/state.rs - TUI state management, panel navigation
- rch/src/tui/app.rs - Event handling, keyboard shortcuts
- rch/src/tui/event.rs - Input event processing

## Success Criteria
- All terminals display correctly (no mojibake/boxes)
- Help overlay documents ALL available shortcuts
- Vim users can navigate with h/j/k/l
- Worker actions available via single keystrokes
- Color blind modes tested and verified","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:58:46.164294374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:19:09.715658331Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:19:09.715590944Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x1ek","title":"Idea: Retry/backoff for rsync + SSH exec","description":"## Background
Transient network failures and SSH hiccups cause unnecessary fallback to local builds. Simple retry with backoff improves reliability without risking long stalls.

## Goals
- Retry rsync upload/download and SSH command execution on transient errors.
- Limit retries and total time to preserve responsiveness.
- Emit clear logs with retry counts and reasons.

## Design / Approach
- Introduce retry policy in transfer pipeline (max_attempts, base_delay, jitter).
- Classify retryable errors (timeout, connection reset) vs non-retryable (auth, host key).
- Respect overall timeouts and fail-open if exceeded.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add retry policy to config with safe defaults.
- Implement retry wrapper for rsync + SSH exec.
- Update mock transport to simulate retryable failures.

## Tests
- Unit: retryable error classification.
- Integration: mock transport fails once then succeeds.
- E2E: timeouts do not exceed configured caps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Transient failures recover without manual retry.
- Non-retryable errors still fail fast.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-x1ek.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Only retry when command definitely did NOT start (connect/transport errors).
- Exponential backoff with jitter and total time cap.
- Non-retryable errors (auth/host key) fail fast.

## E2E Outline
- Mock rsync fails once (retryable) then succeeds.
- Mock auth failure -> no retry.
- Logs include attempt count + total elapsed time.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch-common/src/ssh.rs: retryable error classification.
- rch/src/transfer.rs: retry policy caps total time.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-x1ek.sh: mock rsync fails once then succeeds.
- Verify non-retryable errors fail fast.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:53:29.303781068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:57:06.731964507Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:57:06.731899065Z","close_reason":"Completed: RetryConfig with exponential backoff + jitter, retry logic for both real and mock rsync paths, E2E test script passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
